mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 10%, severe labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings collapses, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 10%, or the signal loses predictive power with p-values above 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which resolves inventory bottlenecks and drives positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations during recovery phases, or regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and wage growth, which immediately boosts market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a frequency exceeding 50%, or rolling Rank IC remains consistently below 0.015.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.5% MoM threshold are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macro and micro channels, reducing recession risk and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit over additive proxies in F-test validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during liquidity crises signal severe supply chain breakdowns and immediate earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during crisis regimes fail to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
